Source: Amazon Q Developer Doc History
URL: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/doc-history.html
Fetched: 2026-03-22

# Document History for Amazon Q Developer User Guide

## Most Recent Updates (February 2026 - Present)

**February 18, 2026** - Updated account limit for subscriptions
- You can now subscribe users in a maximum of 20 accounts per AWS Region, per organization (increased from 10)

**January 16, 2026** - Deprecated code transformation for GitLab
- Code transformation capabilities for Java modernization deprecated for GitLab Duo with Amazon Q Developer
- `/q transform` quick action and CI/CD pipeline customization documentation removed

**January 13, 2026** - Removed deprecated GitLab quick actions
- Removed `/q dev (revise)` and Unit test generation section with `/q test` command from GitLab quick actions documentation

**December 29, 2025** - Updated GitHub feature development workflow
- Conversation-based feedback with `/q` command replaces traditional GitHub review process
- Users provide feedback through natural language commands in pull request conversations

**December 18, 2025** - Updated GitHub app installation registration steps
- Updated steps for navigating Kiro and Amazon Q Developer shared console

**December 15, 2025** - Deprecated code transformation for GitHub
- Code transformation capabilities for Java modernization deprecated for Amazon Q Developer for GitHub

**November 17, 2025** - Q CLI becomes Kiro CLI
- The Q CLI has been renamed to Kiro CLI

**October 29, 2025** - Removed IDE agent documentation
- Agentic chat capabilities replaced `/dev`, `/doc/`, `/test`, and `/review` agents
- Updated code review procedures in the IDE

**October 21, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Added IDE memory bank documentation for generating memory banks for Amazon Q chat
- Added Q CLI feature documentation including agent creation, prompt management, message responses, and project rules
- Placeholder content entry

**September 23, 2025** - Enhanced context management documentation
- Added guidance for choosing the right context approach with comparison table and decision flowchart

**September 18, 2025** - Added Q CLI v1.16.0 feature documentation
- Agent default behavior with priority-based selection
- Legacy MCP configuration support
- New chat commands: `/tangent tail`, `/changelog`
- Agent-related settings documentation

**September 17, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Context hooks deprecated in favor of agent hooks
- Added remote MCP server support with OAuth authentication
- Added built-in tools configuration documentation

**September 9, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Removed Amazon S3 buckets from allowlist table for software development and unit test generation
- New command line tool for transformation version with bug fixes

**September 2, 2025** - Updated Amazon Q Developer for GitHub review agent
- Code reviews include summary with threaded findings
- Added prerequisites: Write, Maintain, or Admin role required in GitHub

**August 21, 2025** - Disabling MCP
- Organizations can now disable MCP

**August 19, 2025** - Updated proxy topic
- Proxy Auto-Configuration (PAC) files are not supported

**August 15, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Added comprehensive troubleshooting documentation
- Added log access and analysis guide
- Updated deployment options topic (max 10 accounts per region)

**August 7, 2025** - Added command line transformation job history
- New `qct history` command documentation

**August 6, 2025** - Added IDE transformation job history topic
- Users can view, manage, and retrieve artifacts from recent Java transformation jobs in VSCode

**August 5, 2025** - GitLab self-managed instance update
- Requires GitLab 17.11.0 or later

**August 1, 2025** - Added code review procedures
- Procedures for running code reviews and addressing code issues using agentic chat in VSCode

**July 31, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Added model selection for IDE chat with available models list
- Added keyboard shortcuts topic across different IDEs and platforms
- Added custom agents feature documentation
- Added chat history compaction topic for context window management

**July 30, 2025** - Added chat history compaction topic

**July 24, 2025** - Added profile deletion topic
- Deleting the Amazon Q Developer profile documentation

**July 21, 2025** - Added opt out steps for telemetry
- Instructions for opting out of telemetry collection while using command line tool for transformation

**July 16, 2025** - Added GitHub workflow for first-party dependencies
- New documentation page for handling first-party dependencies

**July 15, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Updated user activity report metrics with inline chat feature metrics
- Updated proxy configuration for Eclipse, JetBrains, and Visual Studio

**July 9, 2025** - Image context support added to IDE chat
- Users can include images when prompting (UI mockups, sequence diagrams, etc.)

**July 7, 2025** - Updated Builder ID upgrade section
- Cannot link multiple Builder IDs to single AWS account

**July 2, 2025** - Updated profile sharing section
- Clarified purpose and removed incorrect statement about override behavior

**June 30, 2025** - Added slash commands details to GitHub
- `/q` slash commands for feature development, code reviews, code transformation, and help

**June 26, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Added QCT CLI details in IDEs (security, pausing/canceling, new parameters, LLM summary)
- QCT CLI now supports `https://q.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/`
- New content for creating project rules in GitLab and GitHub

**June 25, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Updated KMS policy with `kms:Decrypt`, `kms:DescribeKey`, `kms:Encrypt`, `kms:GenerateDataKey`
- Added proxy configuration instructions for Visual Studio Code

**June 24, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- User activity reports generated at midnight UTC in files with 1,000 users each
- Updated Amazon S3 bucket section (v1.72.0+ doesn't require allowlisting)
- Rebranded Amazon Q Developer operational investigations to CloudWatch investigations

**June 19, 2025** - Updated GitHub feature development and code transformation
- Can be invoked using slash commands in comments within issues

**June 18, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Rules button added to VS Code IDE chat for creating/managing project rules
- Pinned context support added to VS Code for maintaining context across interactions

**June 17, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Updated code transformation details (source/target versions, library upgrades)
- Chat about network security capability added

**June 13, 2025** - Added data storage topic
- Information about where content is stored

**June 12, 2025** - MCP in the IDE
- MCP servers can be used with Amazon Q Developer in the IDE

**June 11, 2025** - Personal account upgrade
- Builder IDs can upgrade to Pro tier

**June 9, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Updated subscription statuses documentation
- Support for specifying dependencies for CLI transformations with dependency upgrade file
- Expanded command line reference with argument details
- Model selection for command line chat

**June 5, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Chat context support expanded to JetBrains, Visual Studio, and Eclipse IDEs
- Agentic chat available in all supported IDEs

**June 2, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Updated GitHub code review details (slash commands in PR comments)
- Cost optimization capabilities in chat

**May 22, 2025** - Updated firewall page
- Added/updated URLs for firewall configuration

**May 21, 2025** - MCP configuration commands
- Commands to configure MCP servers from non-Q command line

**May 16, 2025** - Updated customization policy topic
- Permission errors may occur when creating customization policy

**May 15, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Updated GitHub/GitLab code review behavior (triggered on new/reopened PRs/MRs, not subsequent commits)
- Updated VPC endpoints
- Improved MCP server loading (background loading)
- Added image support in chat using `fs_read` tool
- Added conversation persistence with `/export` and `/import` commands
- Updated managed policies (AmazonQFullAccess, AmazonQDeveloperAccess)

**May 14, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Support for multiple conversations in console chat
- Save and switch between conversations feature

**May 13, 2025** - Added subscription details topic

**May 12, 2025** - Transformation website removed

**May 10, 2025** - Updated terminology
- "identity-aware console sessions" changed to "identity-enhanced console sessions"

**May 8, 2025** - Added example SCP
- Service control policy denying access outside EU regions

**May 5, 2025** - Amazon Q Developer for GitHub
- New chapter with concepts, procedures, setup, and configuration

**May 3, 2025** - Added context hooks support

**May 2, 2025** - Managed policy update
- Permission added to AmazonQFullAccess

**May 1, 2025** - Agentic chat in the IDE
- Agentic chat functionality available in the IDE

**April 30, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Customizations support additional languages
- Managed policies updated (AmazonQFullAccess, GitLabDuoWithAmazonQPermissionsPolicy)
- Added MCP support in CLI

**April 28, 2025** - Added upgrade details
- Information about upgrading from Free to Pro tier

**April 21, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Conversation history saved in IDE chat
- Code as context support (classes, functions, global variables)
- GitLab Duo with Amazon Q updates (onboarding and permissions policy)

**April 15, 2025** - Updated dashboard permissions

**April 13, 2025** - Improved security documentation for command line
- Reorganized with comprehensive guidance on security considerations

**April 12, 2025** - Enhanced command-line security
- New command-line settings section
- Enhanced tool permissions documentation

**April 10, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Inline chat available in Eclipse
- Amazon Q Developer profiles available in Europe (Frankfurt) Region
- `/tools` feature added to CLI for tool permissions management
- Support for natural languages other than English

**April 8, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- GitLab Duo with Amazon Q updates (inline policy, CMK policy, `/fix` removed)
- Email notifications for transformations

**April 4, 2025** - New context, prompt, and project rules topics
- Added documentation for adding context, saving prompts, creating project rules

**April 4, 2025** - Updates to subscriptions topics
- Corrections to understanding subscriptions, aggregated subscriptions, and profile sharing

**March 25, 2025** - Example policy update
- Added `sso:CreateInstance` permission

**March 20, 2025** - Support for C++ and C# in customizations

**March 13, 2025** - Updates to chatting about resources
- Can chat about multiple AWS resources and services

**March 12, 2025** - Additional language support for documentation generation
- C++ and C# support added

**March 6, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- New subscription-related limit (max 50 accounts in organization)
- Context integration to CLI chat

**February 28, 2025** - Policy correction
- JSON syntax error corrected

**February 28, 2025** - New version of command line tool for transformation
- Support for authenticating with IAM through AWS CLI

**February 25, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Customizations policy update (permission added)
- New dashboard topic with metrics descriptions

**February 21, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- New cross-region processing topic
- Managed policy update (AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions)

**February 20, 2025** - `/doc` enhancement
- Can generate infrastructure diagrams

**February 19, 2025** - Multiple updates:
- Amazon Q Developer in chat applications chapter
- Support for Java 21 transformations

**February 14, 2025** - New firewall topic

**February 12, 2025** - New version of command line tool
- Support for converting embedded SQL in Java applications

**February 10, 2025** - Update to retention period
- Transformed code retained for 30 days (up from 24 hours)

**February 7, 2025** - New subscription workflow

**February 3, 2025** - `/dev` enhancement
- Test code it generates

**January 31, 2025** - Customizations section update
- Can include any number of repositories

**January 24, 2025** - Prompt logging examples

**January 23, 2025** - CloudZero plugin
- Available in Amazon Q chat

**January 15, 2025** - User activity report update
- New metrics added

---

## Earlier Updates (2024 and Prior)

**December 16, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Dashboard update (old dashboard removed, filters/metrics added)
- Troubleshooting with Amazon Q section added

**December 13, 2024** - Identity-enhanced sessions update

**December 6, 2024** - New test generation agent

**December 3, 2024** - Multiple major updates:
- Large-scale transformation (.NET, mainframe, VMware workloads)
- GitLab Duo with Amazon Q documentation
- Documentation generation in the IDE
- Code reviews in the IDE (security scans rebranded)
- .NET transformation in the IDE (Visual Studio, preview)
- Transformation on the command line (preview)

**November 27, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Multiple diffs for transformation in the IDE
- Amazon Q in Eclipse (preview)
- Cost analysis now generally available

**November 22, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Dashboard update with new metrics
- CodeConnections repositories in customizations

**November 21, 2024** - Amazon Q command line supports Linux
- Ubuntu 22 and 24 support
- GNOME v42+ or Xorg display with IBus input method framework

**November 20, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Customization in chat now generally available
- New file types supported: `.md`, `.mdx`, `.rst`, `.txt`

**November 18, 2024** - Multiple major updates:
- Languages added: Dart, Lua, R, Swift, SystemVerilog, PowerShell, expanded JSON/YAML
- Customer managed key support
- Cross-region inference topic
- Pro tier quotas section
- Updated AmazonQFullAccess policy

**November 13, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Updated AmazonQDeveloperAccess policy
- Amazon Q plugins feature
- User activity reports enabled

**November 8, 2024** - Customizations section update

**November 5, 2024** - Clarified Pro section

**November 1, 2024** - Inline chat feature

**October 29, 2024** - Updated managed policies

**October 28, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Customizations correction (folder in S3, not bucket root)
- Prompt logging clarification

**October 24, 2024** - Amazon S3 bucket policy fix

**October 22, 2024** - Expanded features chapter

**October 3, 2024** - Multiple major updates:
- Console-to-Code now generally available
- New policy for Amazon Q CLI with AWS CloudShell

**October 2, 2024** - Prompt logging feature

**September 16, 2024** - Setup content updated and restructured

**August 15, 2024** - CodeWhisperer endpoint needed for IDE VPC access

**July 18, 2024** - New endpoint
- Endpoints can use string `q` instead of `codewhisperer`

**July 12, 2024** - Customizations are GA

**July 10, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Customizations (preview)
- Updated AmazonQFullAccess policy

**July 9, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- New AmazonQDeveloperAccess managed policy
- Updated Amazon Q Developer admin policy (added `sso:ListProfiles`)

**June 19, 2024** - Multiple updates:
- Trusted access section
- Updated setup procedures with account instances support

**June 6, 2024** - Updated code examples
- Added C and C++, improved C# examples
- Updated AmazonQFullAccess policy

**April 30, 2024** - Major updates:
- New service-linked role: AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions
- New service-linked role: AWSServiceRoleForAmazonQDeveloper
- New managed policy: AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptionPolicy
- New managed policy: AWSServiceRoleForAmazonQDeveloperPolicy
- **GA release** - Amazon Q Developer available for general audiences
- Amazon CodeWhisperer merged into Amazon Q Developer
- Service and guide renamed to Amazon Q Developer

**March 29, 2024** - Service rename

**March 5, 2024** - New data protection topic
- Content used for service improvement purposes

**January 25, 2024** - New topic
- Instructions for adding Amazon Q to Slack and Microsoft Teams

**January 18, 2024** - New topic

**November 28, 2023** - Preview release
- Initial preview release of Amazon Q Developer User Guide
